topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an irritating constancy that mirrored the knot tightening in my stomach. I was reviewing the quarterly budget projections, a task that usually offered a sense of calm order, but today, every number felt skewed. Then I saw the compensation breakdown for the recent project allocation. My eyes snagged on the figure listed for Alex, the new intern who had been shadowing us for the last month. The disparity was stark, a gulf between his listed salary and what I was earning for comparable work. I leaned closer to the screen, my knuckles whitening around the mouse. A slow, deliberate breath escaped my lips. I forced myself to look away, focusing intently on a spreadsheet column, but the image of that larger number kept replaying behind my eyelids.

[story 2]

"Just double-check those figures, Sarah," Mark said, leaning over my shoulder, his voice casual as he pointed to the HR portal. I nodded, my throat suddenly dry. As I cross-referenced the data, a cold prickle ran down my spine. Alex’s pay stub, which he had carelessly left on his desk, was right there. The amount was significantly higher than my own. I felt a strange tightness in my chest, a pressure building behind my ribs. I cleared my throat, trying to keep my tone even, but I noticed my hands were trembling slightly as I typed the comparison into a separate document. I needed to understand what was going on, but the urge to confront anyone felt monumental, paralyzing me instead.

[story 3]

I spent the afternoon meticulously organizing files, a deliberate attempt to channel my unease into productive motion. Yet, my focus kept drifting back to the data. Every time I glanced toward Alex’s workstation, a flicker of unease would pass over my features—a quick narrowing of the eyes, a subtle stiffening of the shoulders. When the final comparison landed on my monitor, the silence in the small cubicle seemed amplified. I picked up my coffee mug, the ceramic warm against my palm, and stared into the dark liquid as if searching for an answer. My jaw clenched so hard I could feel the strain in my neck. This imbalance felt wrong, deeply unsettling, and I couldn't shake the feeling that something was deliberately hidden.
